class.name: FmodAudioEffectHardLimiter
class.brief_description: Hard limiter to prevent audio clipping and distortion.
class.description: FmodAudioEffectHardLimiter is a brick-wall limiter that prevents the audio signal from exceeding a specified ceiling level. It ensures that the output never goes above the threshold, effectively preventing clipping and distortion that can occur when signals exceed 0 dBFS. Unlike a compressor which gradually reduces gain, a hard limiter applies instantaneous limiting to peaks, making it ideal for final output protection and maximizing loudness without distortion. The limiter can also apply pre-gain to boost the signal before limiting, allowing you to maximize perceived loudness while staying within safe levels.

members.member: The maximum output level in decibels (dB). Range: -12.0 dB to 0.0 dB. Default: -0.3 dB. The signal will never exceed this level. Setting this slightly below 0 dB (e.g., -0.3 dB) provides safety headroom to prevent inter-sample peaks from clipping.

The gain applied to the signal before limiting, in decibels (dB). Range: -30 dB to +30 dB. Default: 0 dB. Use positive values to boost quiet signals before limiting, which can increase perceived loudness. The limiting stage ensures the final output never exceeds the ceiling regardless of pre-gain.

The release time in seconds. Range: 0.001 to 1.0 seconds. Default: 0.1 seconds. This controls how quickly the limiter stops reducing gain after the signal drops below the ceiling. Longer release times produce smoother results but may reduce transients; shorter times are more transparent but may cause pumping.
